titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University College of Architecture, Art and Planning student projects15-5-1416
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Laura Linke

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University College of Architecture, Art and Planning student projects

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. College of Architecture, Art, and Planning

did.abstract: Volumes containing honors designs and theses; an interscholastic competition; and merit solutions for landscape design, also, student projects and drawings, and photographs of projects.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Volumes containing honors designs and theses; an interscholastic competition; and merit solutions for landscape design, 1912-1922. Also, student projects and drawings, and photographs of projects, 1903-1967; three banners and one plaque for the Inter College Athletic Championships, 1910-1916; approximately 300 student pen and ink sketches, primarily copied from published works; and student projects for the Dept. of City and Regional Planning. Includes drawings of Cornell University and Ithaca buildings, such as Sage College, Boardman and Cascadilla Halls, Llenroc, the Grant Mansion, Giles House and student apartments, as well as proposed residential halls and academic buildings. Also, drawings of historic European and classical structures, and architectural elements. Among the students and architects whose work is represented are Carráere & Hastings, Richard C. Murdock (Cornell University Class of 1927), John Wellborn Root (Cornell University Class of 1909), and Robert J. Eidlitz (Cornell University Class of 1885).

Includes Baird Prize competition "Place Plate" artwork from Design 112, February 1932. The winning design (not included) was used by the Wedgwood Company for a set of twelve plates depicting Cornell scenes.

Drawing on canvas by Richard Andrews Tissington (written RA Tissington), who graduated from the College of Architecture in 1900. According to a 1901 publication of Cornell Alumni News, the drawing, entitled "Design for a Ceiling," was the First Prize Drawing in the Cornell Traveling Fellowship Competition. It was also featured in a exhibit for the Convention of the Architectural League of America May 23-25, 1901. The drawing itself is approximately 48"x60"and is extremely fragile.
Framed rendering of a design for an "Alumni Hall - East Elevation. Cornell University" created as a student project by Samuel R. DeLong, Class of 1895, Architecture.

A 2000 "Praxis2" booklet compiled as the final report of a collaborative Architecture/Planning Course working with the Ithaca Neighborhood Housing Services on a site design for a housing development on West Hill in Ithaca, N.Y.

label.ref: Series III. Interscholastic Competition (Vol. I of I)
Series IV. Merit Solutions, Landscape Design 1912-1922 (Vol. 1-10)
Series VII. Thesis Programs Series VIII. Other 1939-1951

odd.head: General
odd.p: A partial guide of the project titles in the collection and names of their creators is available here, PDF file. A new series 1926-1927 starts on page 126 of the guide.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University College of Architecture, Art and Planning student projects, #15-5-1416. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/2938

did.unittitle: 5" x 7" Envelopes Containing Drawings by Other Students
"Problems in Mechanics" (Scrapbook) (AM W.drawings), Warren .P. Laird, Class in Mechanics, Cornell University
"Housing-Whose Responsibility?" (CRP Conference), Dept. of City and Regional Planning Publications
Dept. of City and Regional Planning Plans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Schohane Valley Townsite, Auburn General Plan

did.unittitle: 4 Folders: Theses on Planning by Various Students, Including Raymond G. Ballard, Donald Beach, Allan Ray Cooper, and Arnold Nye
Miesuse: toward the mesoforms edited by Julián Varas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miesuse is a design research project developed in collaboration with a team of architecture students at Cornell University between January and May 2006. The project is part of an investigation on techniques of formal production in architecture, and it seeks to establish lines of continuity and transformation on the basis of spatial concepts developed by Mies van der Rohe during the period 1920-1945.

did.unittitle: Student work for the National Architectural Accrediting Board
Lysander New City, a project for fourth and fifth year students
Series III. Interscholastic Competition (Vol. I of I)
Series IV. Merit Solutions, Landscape Design 1912-1922 (Vol. 1-10)
Design Problems - In Folders Arranged Chronologically
Baird Prize competition "Place Plate" artwork from Design 112
Gold medal from Fine Arts, Head of Charles Lindbergh, raised, on one side, Lone Eagle flying amid spectres on the other
Medal from Fine Arts, Mountain on one side, "There is no easy way from the earth to the stars", 3 men and a winged horse on the other side
Medal from Fine Arts, dancing Native American with snakes on one side, "HOPI", running Native Americans with snakes on the other side, "Prayer for Rain"
Medal from Fine Arts, raised head of Charles Moore, inscription concerning the Commission for the Fine Arts
Medal from Fine Arts, man sowing grain on one side, "Whatsoever a man soweth", shaft of wheat, branch of thorns and scythe on other side, "that shall he also reap"
Medal from Fine Arts, one woman and 3 men, "La Societe honore et en courages les hautes etudes d'architecture", on the other side, compass, "Societe des Architects"
Medal from Fine Arts, Louisiana purchase expedition, 2 women on one side, eagle on the other
College of Architecture Charles Goodwin Sands Memorial medal
"Mirrors of White Hall", an anonymously printed sheet
"Design for a Ceiling", by Richard Andrews Tissington
Large framed rendering of design for "Alumni Hall - East Elevation, Cornell University", by Samuel R. DeLong
